exercises.title: IMMUTABLE WRITE
exercises.brief: Change one field without touching the original.
promptLines: Define **setField<T, K extends keyof T>(obj: T, key: K, value: T[K]): T** returning a **new** object with `key` set to `value`. Do **not** mutate the input.
The `K extends keyof T` bound means `key` must be one of the object's real keys, and `value` must match that field's type.
starterLines: function setField<T, K extends keyof T>(obj: T, key: K, value: T[K]): T {
// TODO: copy obj, then overwrite key with value
solutionLines: function setField<T, K extends keyof T>(obj: T, key: K, value: T[K]): T {

tests.name: does not mutate input

exercises.hint: Object.assign({}, obj, { [key]: value }) — a fresh object, then the one override.
exercises.lore: Write to the record without leaving prints on the original.

exercises.title: SHELL SPLICE
exercises.brief: Splice two shells into one that carries both.
promptLines: Define **combine<A, B>(a: A, b: B): A & B** that shallow-merges `a` and `b` into one object holding **all** keys of both.
The return type `A & B` is an **intersection**: the result satisfies *both* shapes. On a key collision, **`b` wins**.
starterLines: function combine<A, B>(a: A, b: B): A & B {
// TODO: merge a and b into a new object (b wins on conflict)
solutionLines: function combine<A, B>(a: A, b: B): A & B {

tests.name: b wins on conflict
tests.code: assertEqual(combine({ x: 1, y: 2 }, { x: 9 }), { x: 9, y: 2 });

exercises.hint: Object.assign({}, a, b) — later sources overwrite earlier ones, so b wins.
exercises.lore: Two partial shells, one merged body that answers to both.

exercises.title: COUNTER TICK
exercises.brief: Bump a numeric field by one, immutably.
promptLines: Define **incField<T, K extends keyof T>(obj: T, key: K): T** returning a **new** object where the number at `key` is increased by **1**. Do **not** mutate the input.
`keyof T` lets the caller point at any field by name; here we assume that field holds a number.
starterLines: function incField<T, K extends keyof T>(obj: T, key: K): T {
// TODO: copy obj, then add 1 to obj[key]
solutionLines: function incField<T, K extends keyof T>(obj: T, key: K): T {
return Object.assign({}, obj, { [key]: (obj[key] as unknown as number) + 1 });

tests.name: does not mutate input

exercises.hint: Object.assign({}, obj, { [key]: obj[key] + 1 }) — spread, then override the one field.
exercises.lore: Tick the counter on a copy; the live record stays untouched.

exercises.title: FIELD PURGE
exercises.brief: Drop one key, keep the rest, leave the original intact.
promptLines: Define **without<T, K extends keyof T>(obj: T, key: K): T** returning a **new** object that is a copy of `obj` with `key` **removed**. Do **not** mutate the input.
`K extends keyof T` guarantees you can only purge a key that actually exists.
starterLines: function without<T, K extends keyof T>(obj: T, key: K): T {
// TODO: copy obj into a fresh object, then delete key from the copy
solutionLines: function without<T, K extends keyof T>(obj: T, key: K): T {

tests.name: keeps other keys
tests.code: assertEqual(without({ x: 1, y: 2, z: 3 }, 'y'), { x: 1, z: 3 });

tests.name: does not mutate input

exercises.hint: Copy with Object.assign({}, obj), then delete out[key] from the copy — never the original.
exercises.lore: Scrub one field from the duplicate; the source ledger keeps every byte.

exercises.title: PATCH FOLD
exercises.brief: Fold a stack of partial patches into one record.
promptLines: Define **mergeAll<T>(objs: T[]): T** that folds a list of **partial** records into a single object via spread/merge.
Process left to right so **later entries win** on conflicts. An **empty** list yields `{}`.
starterLines: // TODO: fold every object into one (later wins); empty -> {}

tests.name: folds three
tests.code: assertEqual(mergeAll([{ a: 1 }, { b: 2 }, { c: 3 }]), { a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 });

exercises.hint: Start from {} and Object.assign each object in turn so later patches overwrite earlier ones.
exercises.lore: Stack the patches, fold them down, read the final state off the top.
